# settings — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The settings feature is the owner-only configuration surface for VCSM actors and VPORTs. It is organized into four independent controller stacks (account, privacy, profile, vports) under `settings/`, plus a shared adapter layer and a cross-feature dashboard card entry point at `dashboard/vport/dashboard/cards/settings/`. All write paths must be OWNER-gated; the privacy controller stack has two HIGH-severity deferred ownership gaps (ELEK-002/004). The feature manages account lifecycle (create/soft-delete/hard-delete/restore), actor privacy (private/public toggle, block management), profile display (avatar, bio, banner), and VPORT configuration (business card, directory visibility, social settings).

**Source Path:** apps/VCSM/src/features/settings/
**Engine Path:** None — feature-only (no engines/ imports detected)

## Active Controllers

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| `account/controller/account.controller.js` | Account lifecycle: delete account, soft/hard delete VPORT, restore VPORT | `ctrlHardDeleteVport`: `assertActorOwnsVportActorController` (booking adapter); others rely on RPC SECURITY DEFINER |
| `privacy/controller/actorPrivacy.controller.js` | Get/set actor privacy (private/public toggle) | PARTIAL — self-actor: `callerActorId !== actorId` check then `assertActorOwnsVportActorController`; but callerActorId is caller-supplied with no server-side binding (ELEK-002 DEFERRED) |
| `privacy/controller/Blocks.controller.js` | Block/unblock actors, list blocks, search actors | `callerActorId` string equality check against `actorId`; actor-first vportId resolution |
| `profile/controller/profile.controller.js` | Core profile read/save (user + vport modes) | DAL-level `owner_user_id = auth.uid()` guard on VPORT writes; user profile relies on session RLS |
| `profile/controller/saveProfile.controller.js` | Thin wrapper: save user profile display fields | DAL-level only (delegates to `profile.write.dal`) |
| `profile/controller/authSession.controller.js` | Auth session utility | Session gate |
| `profile/controller/recordProfileMediaAsset.controller.js` | Record profile media asset after upload | Via media adapter |
| `profile/controller/resolveVportIdByActorId.controller.js` | Resolve vportId from actorId | Read-only |
| `vports/controller/listMyVports.controller.js` | List caller's VPORTs | DAL-level (`actor_owners` two-hop join) |
| `vports/controller/getAuthedUserId.controller.js` | Resolve authenticated user ID | Session gate |
| `vports/controller/getProfileActorId.controller.js` | Resolve actorId from profile | Read-only lookup |
| `vports/controller/vportBusinessCard.controller.js` | Publish/unpublish VPORT business card | `assertActorOwnsVportActorController` (booking adapter) |
| `vports/controller/vportBusinessCardSettings.controller.js` | Get/save VPORT business card settings | `assertActorOwnsVportActorController` (booking adapter) |
| `vports/controller/vportDirectoryVisibility.controller.js` | Get/toggle VPORT directory visibility | `assertActorOwnsVportActorController` (booking adapter) + DAL `owner_user_id` defense-in-depth |
| `vports/controller/vportSocialSettings.controller.js` | Get/update VPORT social settings | `assertActorOwnsVportActorController` (booking controller direct) + BLOCKED by TICKET-SUB-010-B |

**Note:** `saveProfile.controller.js` is a thin legacy wrapper around the DAL with no auth gate at the controller layer — superseded by `profile.controller.js` for new writes. No controller is confirmed dead/removed but this thin controller should be reviewed for removal.

## Active DALs

| DAL | Tables | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `account/dal/account.read.dal.js` | `vc.actors` | Read `vport_id` and `id`; explicit columns |
| `account/dal/account.write.dal.js` | `public.profiles` (via RPC), `vport.profiles` (via RPC) | RPCs: `soft_delete_citizen_account`, Edge Function `delete-citizen-account`, `vport.soft_delete_vport`, `vport.restore_vport`, `vport.hard_delete_vport` |
| `privacy/dal/blocks.dal.js` | `moderation.blocks`, `vc.actors` | RPCs: `moderation.block_actor`, `moderation.unblock_actor`; explicit column list |
| `privacy/dal/visibility.dal.js` | `vc.actor_privacy_settings` | UPSERT on conflict `actor_id`; no `auth.getUser()` binding (ELEK-004 DEFERRED) |
| `profile/dal/actorIdBySubject.read.dal.js` | `vc.actors` (inferred) | `dalReadActorIdByProfileId`, `dalReadActorIdByVportId` |
| `profile/dal/actors.read.dal.js` | `vc.actors` | `dalReadVportIdByActorId` — explicit columns |
| `profile/dal/auth.read.dal.js` | Auth session | `getUser()` wrapper |
| `profile/dal/profile.read.dal.js` | `public.profiles`, `vport.profiles` | Explicit columns; dual-mode (user/vport) |
| `profile/dal/profile.write.dal.js` | `public.profiles`, `vport.profiles` | Owner guard: `owner_user_id = auth.uid()` on VPORT; user relies on session-scoped RLS |
| `profile/dal/profileMediaAsset.write.dal.js` | `platform.media_assets` (inferred) | Profile media asset recording |
| `profile/dal/vportPublicDetails.read.dal.js` | `vport.profile_public_details` | Read VPORT public details |
| `profile/dal/vportPublicDetails.write.dal.js` | `vport.profile_public_details` | Upsert VPORT public details; BW-SETTINGS-005 (no optimistic lock) |
| `vports/dal/actorOwners.read.dal.js` | `vc.actor_owners` | Read actor owners; no `auth.getUser()` binding (ELEK-006 INFO) |
| `vports/dal/auth.read.dal.js` | Auth session | `getUser()` wrapper |
| `vports/dal/vports.read.dal.js` | `vc.actor_owners`, `vc.actors`, `vport.profiles` | Two-hop actor_owners join; `readVportBusinessCardSettingsDAL` and `readVportDirectoryStateDAL` use legacy `owner_user_id` guard (VENOM-SETTINGS-004) |
| `vports/dal/vports.write.dal.js` | `vport.profiles`, `vport.profile_public_details` | RPC `set_business_card_publish_state` (SECURITY DEFINER); direct UPDATE with `owner_user_id` guard; `syncDirectoryVisibleToPublicDetailsDAL` extracted from primary write (VPD-V-FIX-002) |

## Engine Dependencies

None — the settings feature has no imports from `engines/`. Cross-platform concerns (actor hydration, identity) are accessed via VCSM internal adapters and state layer.

---

## Cross-Feature Dependencies

| Feature | What Is Imported | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| `booking` | `assertActorOwnsVportActorController` (adapter + direct controller import) | settings → booking (ownership gate) |
| `identity` | `useIdentity`, `useIdentityOps` (adapter) | settings → identity |
| `profiles` | `useProfilesOps` (adapter) | settings → profiles (cache invalidation) |
| `social` | `invalidateActorPrivacyCacheAdapter`, `invalidateActorSocialPublicPolicyCache`, `invalidateActorSocialSettingsCache`, `dalUpdateActorSocialSettings`, `dalGetActorSocialSettings`, `useFollowRequestActions`, `useIncomingFollowRequests` | settings → social (privacy/social settings) |
| `feed` | `invalidateActorBundleEntry` (adapter) | settings → feed (cache invalidation) |
| `actors` | `searchActorsAdapter` | settings → actors (block search) |
| `media` | `createMediaAssetController` (adapter) | settings → media (uploads) |
| `vport` | `CreateVportForm.jsx.adapter`, `vport.adapter` (media asset DALs), `vport.public.adapter` | settings → vport (create form, asset recording) |
| `notifications` | `getUnreadNotificationCount` (adapter) | settings → notifications (badge count) |
| `dashboard/qrcode` | `QrCode` component (adapter) | settings → dashboard qrcode |
| `ads` | `OnemoredaysAd.adapter` | settings → ads |

**Note:** `vportSocialSettings.controller.js` imports directly from `@/features/social/privacy/dal/actorSocialSettings.dal` — a DAL-to-DAL cross-feature import that bypasses the adapter boundary. This is a boundary violation.

---

## Authorization Pattern

The VPORT write paths use a two-layer defense pattern:

1. **Controller layer** — `assertActorOwnsVportActorController` from `@/features/booking/adapters/booking.adapter` (or direct controller import). Verifies `actor_owners` membership, checks `kind='vport'`, and confirms the actor is not void/deleted. This is the primary gate.
2. **DAL layer** — `owner_user_id = auth.uid()` WHERE clause provides defense-in-depth. This is a secondary check.

For account delete operations, ownership is enforced via SECURITY DEFINER RPCs and an Edge Function with service-role access.

For privacy/blocks, `ctrlBlockActor` and `ctrlUnblockActor` use string equality `callerActorId === actorId` as the ownership check. `ctrlSetActorPrivacy` is partially gated: when `callerActorId !== actorId` it calls `assertActorOwnsVportActorController`, but `callerActorId` is caller-supplied and not anchored to the server-side session (ELEK-002 HIGH DEFERRED).

User profile writes rely on session-scoped RLS at the DB level with no explicit controller-layer ownership check.

---

## Module Independence Classification

DEPENDENT

The settings feature has broad cross-feature imports spanning booking (ownership gate), social (privacy/social settings), identity, profiles, feed, actors, media, vport, notifications, ads, and the dashboard. The ownership gate itself (`assertActorOwnsVportActorController`) is owned by the `booking` feature — settings cannot enforce VPORT ownership without it.

---

## Architecture State

EVOLVING

Four controller stacks are present and the VPORT write paths are gated. However the feature is actively mid-sprint: TICKET-SUB-010-B (social settings RLS migration) is pending, two HIGH deferred security gaps remain (ELEK-002/004 on the privacy stack), a deprecated DAL is still live (ELEK-005), a model file sits in a UI folder (layer violation), a direct cross-feature DAL import bypasses the adapter boundary (`actorSocialSettings.dal`), and the `queries/` folder contains 6 hooks of unconfirmed dead-code status.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **ELEK-002 (HIGH, DEFERRED)** — `ctrlSetActorPrivacy` accepts `actorId` from the caller with no server-side session binding; actor privacy hijack is possible for any actorId a caller can supply.
2. **ELEK-004 (HIGH, DEFERRED)** — `dalSetActorPrivacy` has no `auth.getUser()` binding; RLS status on `vc.actor_privacy_settings` is unconfirmed.
3. **ELEK-005 (OPEN)** — `dalDeleteOwnedVportById` is a deprecated DAL still exported live. It uses the legacy `owner_user_id` guard and omits cascade logic. It should be replaced with a throw stub.
4. **VENOM-SETTINGS-004 (P2, DEFERRED)** — `listMyVportsDAL` and `readMyVports` use `owner_user_id` for ownership resolution; should be rewritten to use the canonical `actor_owners` two-hop join.
5. **Layer violation** — `profile/ui/vportAboutDetails.model.js` is a model file inside a UI folder. Must be relocated to `profile/model/`.
6. **Adapter boundary violation** — `vportSocialSettings.controller.js` imports directly from `@/features/social/privacy/dal/actorSocialSettings.dal` instead of going through a social adapter.
7. **Dead-code risk** — `settings/queries/` contains 6 hook files (`useAccountSettings`, `useBlockedCitizens`, `usePrivacySettings`, `useProfileSettings`, `useUpdateVportVisibility`, `useUserVports`) whose live usage status is unconfirmed. May be superseded by controller-pattern hooks in the sub-stacks.
8. **BW-SETTINGS-005 (OPEN)** — `upsertVportPublicDetailsDAL` has no optimistic locking; replay attack after session compromise is possible.
9. **TICKET-SUB-010-B (PENDING)** — `actor_social_settings` owner-delegation RLS migration not applied; blocks `ctrlUpdateVportSocialSettings` from being usable.
10. **Ownership gate imported from `booking`** — `assertActorOwnsVportActorController` is the canonical ownership gate but lives in the `booking` feature. Settings has a structural dependency on `booking` that is not mediated by a shared engine or shared utility.

---

## Final Module Status

MOSTLY_COMPLETE

Core architecture is present and active. VPORT write paths are gated. The feature is blocked at full completeness by: two HIGH deferred security gaps (ELEK-002/004), a pending DB migration (TICKET-SUB-010-B), zero test coverage, a deprecated live DAL, a model in a UI folder, an adapter boundary violation, and unconfirmed dead-code in the `queries/` folder.
